title: Card payments for buskers and street musicians — 0% cut · live.tips
meta_description: Busking without cash? Take card payments from one QR code on the case. Fans scan, pick an amount, leave a message — and every tip lands directly in your own Stripe account. 0% cut, open source.
twitter_description: Card payments for buskers — one QR code on the case, and every tip lands directly in your own Stripe account. 0% cut, open source.
og_image_alt: live.tips — card payments for buskers, on stage in minutes
jsonld_description: Open-source live tip jar for buskers, street musicians and bar bands: take card payments from one QR code on stage, and every tip lands directly in the artist's own Stripe account — 0% cut, no middleman.
hero_badge: Open source · 0% cut · powered by your own Stripe
hero_h1:
Card payments for buskers.
On stage in minutes.
hero_sub: One QR code on the guitar case. Fans scan, pick an amount, leave a name and a message — and every card tip lands directly in your own Stripe account. No platform, no middleman, no cut.
hero_trust: No account, no install — your Stripe key never leaves your device
stage_caption: This is the real renderer, live in your browser — the audience-facing stage every fan sees during a set. Every coin is a real tip pouring in.
how_h2: From soundcheck to your first card tip in three steps
step1_p: Create a restricted API key in your own Stripe dashboard — two minutes, fully guided — and paste it in. The app creates your pay-what-you-want tip link automatically.
step2_p: Print the poster, tape it to the guitar case, put it on the merch table. Fans scan, pick an amount, leave a name and a message — Stripe handles the payment.
step3_p: Your tablet on stage shows every tip in real time against tonight's goal — 3D jar, messages, confetti. Edit the goal mid-set; lock the screen while it sits out front.
alt_pay_p: Optional, alongside Stripe: switch them on and the same QR still opens one tip page listing every method you accept — no separate code per app. Revolut and MobilePay tips appear on your stage screen instantly while the fan pays in their app — shown as unverified, since those apps can't confirm the payment.
sec_lede: With Stripe there is no live.tips server between your fans and your bank account — the app talks to api.stripe.com and nowhere else. Optional Revolut and MobilePay tips go through a tiny open-source relay that stores no payment data.
sec1_h3: Bring your own key, keep your own key
sec1_p: The restricted key is stored in your device's keychain and only ever sent to Stripe over TLS. Full live secret keys are refused outright.
sec2_p: The key can create your tip link and watch donations — nothing else. It cannot touch balances, payouts, refunds, or customer data.
sec3_h3: Refunds and payouts stay in Stripe
sec3_p: Money matters stay where they belong: in your own Stripe dashboard, under your own account.
perm_note: Everything else stays “None” — no payouts, no refunds, no balance access.
mini1_p: A progress bar the whole room can root for. Edit the goal mid-set without stopping the session.
mini2_p: Blocks casual tampering while the device sits out front. Unlock with Face ID or your PIN.
mini3_p: QR posters in several themes and languages, ready for the merch table.
mini4_p: Try a full simulated session before connecting Stripe — no account needed.
cta_p: Runs in your browser today — no install, no signup. Native apps and more payment methods are on the roadmap.
cta_foot: Made for buskers, bar bands and street artists — by one of you.
foot_note: Open source under the MIT license. Tips go straight to the artist's own Stripe account — live.tips never touches the money.
banner: This site is also available in English.
